note: Alaska AI video feed manifest. Newest first. The dispatch routine in alaska-ai-weekly appends a new entry each run via scripts/publish_feed.py. id must be unique (used for dedupe + deep links).

videos.title: Counting Belugas From Orbit
videos.caption: NOAA's experimental GAIA machine vision is learning to find the endangered Cook Inlet belugas, about 331 left, in satellite imagery. The honest open question is whether it can get the clear satellite passes it needs over Anchorage's crowded sky.

videos.title: The Checkpoint Lever
videos.caption: The Air Force offered 4,700 acres across three Alaska bases for AI data centers. Nobody has been picked yet, and the lease is still open.

videos.title: Ear and Eye on the Ice-White Whale
videos.caption: Two NOAA-led AI systems now watch Cook Inlet's endangered belugas from two directions at once, a robot ear underwater and a robot eye in orbit. 331 belugas remain.

videos.title: The Pen That Won't Land
videos.caption: Kenai Peninsula schools adopted an AI policy before the governance to back it up. The pen hovers over the signature line.

videos.title: The Referee Arrives
videos.caption: Alaska's salmon bycatch fight meets the AI that counts the fish. NOAA's model reads trawl net-cam footage, and the Sitka Tribe runs its own AI video weir.

videos.title: Alaska Ran the Sky
videos.caption: The XPRIZE Wildfire autonomous-drone finals were held at Nenana, Alaska. UAF ran the airspace while AI systems raced to detect and put out a fire with no human in the loop.

videos.title: He Paid $50K Just to Wait
videos.caption: Golden Valley Electric weighs a second naphtha turbine at North Pole as new demand, including AI, presses on the Interior grid.

videos.title: 31 Square Miles, No Charge
videos.caption: Alaska DNR's preliminary decision would convey about 19,950 acres of Mat-Su state forest to AIDEA at no charge for a data-center, rail and energy district.

videos.title: The $15M Mineral Hunt
videos.caption: UAF won a $15M NSF award to use AI to prospect Alaska's critical minerals.

videos.title: Hell No on the Tundra
videos.caption: Stak Energy's proposed gigawatt AI data center near Deadhorse drew 500+ public comments, fewer than a dozen in favor. The mailbox is the real vote.

videos.title: The Claim on the Tundra
videos.caption: A gigawatt gas-fired AI data center proposed on a square mile of North Slope state land, with cost estimates spanning a twentyfold range and no confirmed customers.

videos.title: Sorted by a Machine
videos.caption: An LLM screened ~1,100 federal grants and an Alaska Native Language Archive grant was among ~1,400 terminated. A court ruled it unlawful. The model did not decide, a person aimed it.

videos.title: The Fake That Swam
videos.caption: An AI-generated image claimed a beluga escaped Seward's SeaLife Center. Nearly 6 million views before the debunk, and the detector could only say 82.6 percent likely AI.

videos.title: The Lid
videos.caption: A UAF model bias-corrects wildfire smoke forecasts for Alaska, where an inversion lid traps smoke low and the forecast paints it thin and high.

videos.title: Teaching the Machine to See
videos.caption: On Bristol Bay's Wood River, a person hand-labels thousands of sockeye to teach a drone's computer vision while the ADFG towers keep the official count.

videos.title: The Mountain Listens
videos.caption: VOISS-Net, an Alaska Volcano Observatory deep-learning model, reads a volcano's seismic and infrasound spectrograms while Great Sitkin erupts. It names the sound. A human stays on watch.

videos.title: The Count From a Dot
videos.caption: A weakly-supervised model counts Alaska caribou in aerial survey photos from a single point per animal, validated on ADFG's Central Arctic census.

videos.title: The Launch Call
videos.caption: ANTHC and Stanford built a decision AI for rural Alaska medevac calls, with tribal data governance at the center.

videos.title: The Depth Model
videos.caption: A UAF probabilistic model predicts where Chinook salmon swim in the water column, to help pollock trawlers avoid them.

videos.title: The Whale Spotter
videos.caption: An AI eye for spotting whales in Alaska waters.

videos.title: The Ten-Minute Clock
videos.caption: At the XPRIZE Wildfire finals in Nenana, autonomous AI drone systems had ten minutes to find and suppress a controlled wildfire. No human in the loop.

videos.title: The Circuit
videos.caption: The Air Force offered 4,700 acres for AI data centers while Cook Inlet's gas squeeze leaves the Interior grid straining to feed them.

videos.title: The Razor Edge
videos.caption: An ML model pulled about 1,750 uncatalogued quakes out of a decade of Alaska seismic noise, tracing a 250 km buried plate edge under southern Alaska.

videos.title: The River Sonar
videos.caption: Kenai River salmon counted by imaging sonar and computer vision.

videos.title: The Digital Twin
videos.caption: A permafrost digital twin under a road in Utqiagvik, built from physics-informed ML and fiber-optic sensing.
